maml:description.maml:para: Generates a pseudonymized diagnostic report suitable for pasting into a GitHub issue or support request.

Collects environment information relevant to CpmfUipsPack installation and operation. All personal identifiers (username, computer name, domain) are replaced with placeholders. Environment variable values are never emitted - only whether each UIPS_* variable is set or not.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Prints a report block to the console. Copy and paste the output directly into https://github.com/rpapub/cpmf-uips-pwshpack/issues

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads and installs uipcli and its required .NET runtime into the user profile (%LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\tools). No admin rights required. Idempotent: already-present artifacts are detected and skipped.
For uipcli 23.x (classic): downloads .NET 6.0.36 (base + WindowsDesktop) and extracts uipcli from its NuGet package.
For uipcli 25.x+ (dotnet tool): downloads the .NET 8 SDK and installs uipcli via `dotnet tool install` into the user profile.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
UiPath CLI version to install. Defaults to 23.10.2.6. Versions matching '^23\.' use the classic nupkg extraction path. All other versions use the dotnet tool install path.
Root directory for all installed tools. Defaults to %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\tools.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
UiPath CLI version to install. Defaults to 23.10.2.6. Versions matching '^23\.' use the classic nupkg extraction path. All other versions use the dotnet tool install path.
Root directory for all installed tools. Defaults to %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\tools.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Scaffolds the user-level CpmfUipsPack config file at the XDG-inspired location: %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\CpmfUipsPack\config.psd1
Copies the bundled examples\uipath-pack.psd1 to the user config directory. All keys in the file are commented examples - edit the file to activate them.
The user config is the lowest-priority config source. It is overridden by: env vars (UIPS_*) > -ConfigFile > explicit parameters
Overwrite an existing config file. Without -Force, the command does nothing if the target file already exists.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Overwrite an existing config file. Without -Force, the command does nothing if the target file already exists.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Installs a git pre-push hook that calls Invoke-CpmfUipsPack -UseWorktree automatically before every push.
Writes a pre-push hook script to <RepoRoot>/.git/hooks/pre-push with LF line endings and no file extension (required by Git for Windows).
The hook calls Invoke-CpmfUipsPack with -UseWorktree so it packs from a clean worktree, never touching the working directory.
ProjectJson is written as an absolute path at install time. If you move or re-clone the repository, re-run Install-CpmfUipsPackGitHook.
Root of the git repository. Defaults to the current directory.
Absolute path to the UiPath project.json. Written verbatim into the hook.

Path to CpmfUipsPack.psd1. Written verbatim into the hook so the hook can Import-Module without relying on the user's PSModulePath. Defaults to the path of the currently loaded CpmfUipsPack module manifest.
Extra arguments appended to the Invoke-CpmfUipsPack call in the hook, e.g. @('-SkipInstall', '-FeedPath', 'C:\myfeed').
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Root of the git repository. Defaults to the current directory.
Absolute path to the UiPath project.json. Written verbatim into the hook.

Path to CpmfUipsPack.psd1. Written verbatim into the hook so the hook can Import-Module without relying on the user's PSModulePath. Defaults to the path of the currently loaded CpmfUipsPack module manifest.
Extra arguments appended to the Invoke-CpmfUipsPack call in the hook, e.g. @('-SkipInstall', '-FeedPath', 'C:\myfeed').
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill in the Synopsis }}
{{ Fill in the Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the uipcli and .NET runtime tool directories and cleans DOTNET_ROOT and the associated PATH entry from the user environment registry.
{{ Fill in the Description }}
UiPath CLI version to remove. Defaults to 23.10.2.6.
Root directory used during installation. Defaults to %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\tools.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
UiPath CLI version to remove. Defaults to 23.10.2.6.
Root directory used during installation. Defaults to %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\tools.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the user-level CpmfUipsPack config file at the XDG-inspired location: %LOCALAPPDATA%\cpmf\CpmfUipsPack\config.psd1
Complements Install-CpmfUipsPackConfig. Removes the config file and, if the parent directory is empty afterwards, removes the directory too.
Suppress the confirmation prompt. Without -Force, ShouldProcess governs whether the deletion proceeds (respects -WhatIf and -Confirm).
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Suppress the confirmation prompt. Without -Force, ShouldProcess governs whether the deletion proceeds (respects -WhatIf and -Confirm).
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Reads projectVersion from a UiPath project.json, increments it, writes it back, and returns the new version string.

Version bump rules: - Prerelease suffix with numeric tail (1.2.3-alpha.4) → alpha.5 - Prerelease suffix without numeric (1.2.3-alpha) → alpha.1 - Build metadata with numeric tail (1.2.3+build.4) → build.5 - Plain release (1.2.3) → 1.3.0 (minor bump)
With -NoBump, the file is not modified; the current version is returned.
Path to the UiPath project.json file.
Return the current version without writing any changes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Path to the UiPath project.json file.
Return the current version without writing any changes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: [string] The new (or current, if -NoBump) version string.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}
